title: CMB Power Spectrum Parameter Estimation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: CMB Power Spectrum Parameter Estimation: CMB parameter estimation: infer cosmological parameters from CMB temperature and polarization power spectra via Boltzmann code. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.boltzmann.transfer…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~200; foreground_contamination, beam_uncertainty_percent set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: C_l^{TT,EE,TE}(theta) from CAMB/CLASS Boltzmann solver; chi2 = sum_l (2l+1)/2 [C_l ** obs/C_l ** th - 1 - ln(C_l ** obs/C_l ** th)]
E.world_state_x: cosmological parameter vector theta = (Omega_b, Omega_c, h, A_s, n_s, tau)
E.observation_y: CMB angular power spectra C_l^{TT,EE,TE,BB} from l=2 to l_max

W.regime: Existence of the recovered cosmological_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 200); foreground_contamination dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Thermal gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: sigma(Omega_c) <= 0.01 at Planck noise level. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~200. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |foreground_contamination|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_CAMB * N_MCMC) with N_CAMB ~ 1s per call per iteration
